page: OVHcloud b2-60 is a general-purpose virtual server instance built on the KVM hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated physical cores powered by an Intel Core Processor of the Haswell architecture, running at a base frequency of 2.0 GHz without TSX support. The system is equipped with 60.0 GB of RAM, providing 3.75 GB of memory per core, and includes 400 GB of local SSD storage. Network connectivity is established with a baseline bandwidth of 1 Gbps, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Performance benchmarks reveal a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core processing is relatively weak, as evidenced by bottom-quartile scores in stress-ng single-core, Geekbench single-core, and single-threaded bzip3 decompression tests. This is primarily due to the older processor architecture and modest clock speed. Conversely, multi-core performance is average, with competitive marks in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, Geekbench multi-core, and software compilation. Memory performance follows a similar pattern, where read bandwidth is average but large-block write bandwidth falls into the bottom 25%.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the b2-60 offers dedicated physical resources and a balanced memory-to-vCPU ratio, making it a stable option for workloads that can leverage parallel processing without requiring cutting-edge single-thread speeds. It is well-suited for multi-threaded applications, static web serving, database operations, and software compilation. However, it is less optimal for single-threaded tasks, memory-write-heavy operations, or large-scale LLM text generation.
description: OVHcloud b2-60 is a general-purpose virtual server featuring 16 dedicated physical cores on an Intel Haswell architecture running at 2.0 GHz. It is configured with 60.0 GB of RAM, 400 GB of local SSD storage, and a 1 Gbps baseline network interface. Benchmark data indicates a performance tradeoff: single-core CPU execution and large-block memory write bandwidth are weak, falling into the bottom 25% of tested servers. However, multi-core performance, memory read bandwidth, and database operations perform at an average tier. This hardware profile makes the b2-60 qualitatively cost-effective for parallelized workloads that do not demand high single-thread throughput. It is best suited for multi-threaded applications, software compilation, static web serving, and moderate database operations.
og_description: OVHcloud b2-60 is a general-purpose KVM virtual server featuring 16 dedicated Intel cores, 60 GB RAM, and 400 GB SSD. It delivers average multi-core performance but has weak single-core throughput.
meta_description: OVHcloud b2-60 is a general-purpose KVM server with 16 dedicated Intel cores, 60 GB RAM, and 400 GB SSD, suitable for multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A dedicated 16-core virtual server with balanced memory and local SSD storage, optimized for parallelized general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated physical Intel cores at 2.0 GHz
60.0 GB of system memory and 400 GB of local SSD storage
Average multi-core CPU and memory read performance
Weak single-core processing and large-block memory write speeds
1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with one public IPv4 address
Best suited for multi-threaded applications, web serving, and databases
